instructions.name: accept_challenge
docs: PVP: Challenger accepts the challenge by matching the bet amount (REKT HIM).
TEAM: A participant joins either the creator's side or the opponent's side.
`params.join_creator_side` is only meaningful for TEAM challenges:
- true  → join the creator's side
- false → join the opponent's side
For PVP challenges this field is ignored.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: challenger_usdc_account
docs: Challenger's USDC token account (source of the matching bet)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

instructions.name: admin_cancel_challenge
docs: Admin-only: force-cancel any Open or Active challenge, regardless of
whether a PVP challenger has accepted or TEAM opponents have joined.
The creator's own bet is refunded immediately; every other depositor
(PVP challenger, TEAM creator_team/opponent_team members) reclaims
their own stake afterward via `claim_refund`.

accounts.name: admin
docs: The admin wallet — only `Config::admin` may force-cancel a challenge.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: creator_usdc_account
docs: Creator's USDC token account (refund destination for their own bet)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

accounts.name: rent_payer
docs: Wallet that paid this challenge's rent at creation (`challenge.rent_payer`,
enforced via `has_one` on `challenge` above) — receives the reclaimed
vault + challenge PDA rent. Distinct from `admin`, which only gates who
may force-cancel.

instructions.name: admin_withdraw
docs: Emergency backstop: withdraw USDC from any challenge's vault to any
recipient token account, in any amount up to the vault balance,
regardless of challenge status. For cases where a participant cannot
complete the normal settle/claim flow themselves.
Gated on the dedicated `WithdrawAuthority` PDA (see
`initialize_withdraw_authority` / `update_withdraw_authority`), not
`Config::admin` — this instruction has full custody of every escrowed
vault, so it is signed by a wallet kept deliberately separate from the
hot admin key used elsewhere.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must match `withdraw_authority.authority` — a dedicated wallet kept
separate from `Config::admin`.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: recipient_usdc_account
docs: Destination for the withdrawn USDC — any token account chosen by the
admin (e.g. the affected participant's own wallet, verified off-chain).

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

instructions.name: cancel_challenge
docs: Creator cancels an unaccepted challenge and reclaims their bet.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: creator_usdc_account
docs: Creator's USDC token account (refund destination)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

accounts.name: rent_payer
docs: Wallet that paid this challenge's rent at creation (`challenge.rent_payer`,
enforced via `has_one` on `challenge` above) — receives the reclaimed
vault + challenge PDA rent.

instructions.name: claim_refund
docs: Any non-creator depositor on a Cancelled challenge reclaims their own stake —
a PVP challenger, or a TEAM creator_team/opponent_team member. Covers refunds
left over after both `cancel_challenge` (creator self-cancel) and
`admin_cancel_challenge` (admin force-cancel); the creator's own refund is
always issued directly by whichever of those two cancelled the challenge.
Each participant can only call this once (enforced by the ClaimRecord PDA).

accounts.name: participant
docs: The creator-side participant claiming their refund

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: participant_usdc_account
docs: Participant's USDC token account (refund destination)

accounts.name: claim_record
docs: Claim record — created here; its existence prevents double-claiming.
Reuses the same seed namespace as claim_winnings; Cancelled vs Settled
status guarantees there is no collision between the two instructions.

accounts.name: rent_payer
docs: Wallet that paid this challenge's rent at creation (`challenge.rent_payer`,
enforced via `has_one` on `challenge` above) — receives the reclaimed
vault + challenge PDA rent.

instructions.name: claim_winnings
docs: TEAM mode only: a winner on the winning side claims their proportional
share of the pot after the challenge has been settled.
Each participant can only call this once (enforced by the ClaimRecord PDA).

accounts.name: participant
docs: The participant claiming their winnings

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: participant_usdc_account
docs: Participant's USDC token account (payout destination)

accounts.name: claim_record
docs: Claim record — created here; its existence prevents double-claiming.
Seeds: [b"claim", challenge.key(), participant.key()]

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint

accounts.name: rent_payer
docs: Wallet that paid this challenge's rent at creation (`challenge.rent_payer`,
enforced via `has_one` on `challenge` above) — receives the reclaimed
vault + challenge PDA rent once the final winner has claimed.

instructions.name: create_challenge
docs: Creator posts a new challenge, locking their SOL bet into escrow.
Specify `challenge_type = ChallengeType::Pvp` for a 1-vs-1 challenge or
`ChallengeType::Team` for a multi-participant team challenge.

accounts.name: fee_payer
docs: Pays the SOL rent for account creation — either `creator` themself
(self-pay, when they have enough SOL) or `config.admin` (sponsored).
Chosen client-side by checking the creator's SOL balance before
building this instruction. Stored as `challenge.rent_payer` so every
later close instruction refunds rent to whichever of the two actually
paid here, instead of unconditionally to admin.

accounts.name: creator_counter
docs: Per-creator counter — init on first challenge, increment thereafter

accounts.name: challenge
docs: The challenge account itself. Sized dynamically to this challenge's
own requested TEAM roster (see `effective_team_size`) instead of
always reserving room for the platform-wide `MAX_TEAM_SIZE` cap —
keeps rent proportional to what the challenge actually needs.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — an ATA owned by the challenge PDA.
Holds all bets (creator's + all participants').

accounts.name: creator_usdc_account
docs: Creator's USDC token account (source of the bet)

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — must match the devnet USDC address

instructions.name: initialize_config
docs: One-time bootstrap: creates the global `Config` PDA. Only the genesis
`ADMIN_PUBKEY` can call this; afterwards `Config::admin` (mutable via
`update_admin`) is the live source of admin authority.

instructions.name: initialize_withdraw_authority
docs: One-time bootstrap: creates the `WithdrawAuthority` PDA and sets the
dedicated wallet that will be the sole signer for `admin_withdraw`
going forward. Callable only by `Config::admin`; after this call
succeeds, `Config::admin` has no further authority over
`admin_withdraw` — only `WithdrawAuthority::authority` (rotatable via
`update_withdraw_authority`) does.

instructions.name: settle_challenge
docs: Admin/oracle settles the challenge after expiry.
PVP: winner payout, platform fee, and creator-revenue fee are all sent
directly on-chain.
TEAM: platform fee and creator-revenue fee are paid immediately; the
winning side is recorded and each winner must call
`claim_winnings` separately to pull their proportional share.

accounts.name: admin
docs: The admin/oracle wallet that is authorised to settle challenges.
In production this should be a multisig or a Switchboard/Pyth oracle CPI.

accounts.name: challenger
docs: For TEAM challenges pass any pubkey (e.g. creator again) — it won't be paid out here.

accounts.name: vault
docs: USDC vault token account — owned by the challenge PDA

accounts.name: winner_usdc_account
docs: PVP only: winner's USDC token account — must actually belong to whichever
side `creator_wins` declares as the winner (creator or challenger).
For TEAM challenges pass any valid USDC token account — it won't be written to.
`dup`: when the creator wins, this is the same account as
`creator_usdc_account` (both are the creator's ATA). That's safe to alias —
SPL token accounts are owned by the token program, not this one, so Anchor
never re-serializes them on exit; balance changes only happen via the
`transfer_checked` CPIs below, which are correct regardless of aliasing.

accounts.name: treasury_usdc_account
docs: Platform treasury USDC token account (receives the fee for both PVP and TEAM)

accounts.name: creator_usdc_account
docs: Creator-revenue USDC token account — receives creator_fee_bps of the pot
for BOTH PVP and TEAM, regardless of who wins. Required in both cases
(unlike winner_usdc_account's "dummy for TEAM" pattern), since this cut
is paid unconditionally on every settlement.

accounts.name: usdc_mint
docs: USDC mint — validated by token account constraints above

accounts.name: rent_payer
docs: Wallet that paid this challenge's rent at creation (`challenge.rent_payer`,
enforced via `has_one` on `challenge` above) — receives the vault +
challenge PDA rent refund when a PVP settlement fully closes them out.
Unused for TEAM (nothing closes here; the last `claim_winnings` caller
closes them instead).

instructions.name: update_admin
docs: Admin-only: rotate the platform wallet that sponsors SOL fees and is
authorised to settle challenges / update platform parameters.

instructions.name: update_platform_params
docs: Admin-only: tune platform_fee_bps / creator_fee_bps / min_bet_amount /
max_duration_secs / min_duration_secs / max_team_size at runtime, without
a redeploy. Unset fields in `args` are left unchanged.

instructions.name: update_withdraw_authority
docs: Rotates the dedicated `admin_withdraw` signer. Callable only by the
*current* withdraw authority itself, not by `Config::admin` — keeps a
compromised admin hot wallet from ever reassigning this authority to
itself.

errors.name: BetTooSmall
errors.msg: Bet amount is below the minimum allowed

errors.name: DurationTooShort
errors.msg: Challenge duration is too short (minimum 5 minutes)

errors.name: DurationTooLong
errors.msg: Challenge duration is too long (maximum 7 days)

errors.name: AlreadyAccepted
errors.msg: Challenge has already been accepted

errors.name: NotOpen
errors.msg: Challenge is not in Open status

errors.name: NotActive
errors.msg: Challenge is not in Active status

errors.name: NotExpired
errors.msg: Challenge has not yet expired

errors.name: ChallengeExpired
errors.msg: Challenge has already expired; cannot accept

errors.name: CannotAcceptOwnChallenge
errors.msg: Creator cannot accept their own challenge

errors.name: UnauthorizedCancel
errors.msg: Only the challenge creator can cancel

errors.name: UnauthorizedSettle
errors.msg: Only the admin/oracle can settle challenges

errors.name: Overflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: AssetTooLong
errors.msg: Asset symbol too long (max 10 chars)

errors.name: InvalidResolvesAt
errors.msg: Resolve time must be after expiry time

errors.name: TeamFull
errors.msg: Team is full; max_team_size has been reached

errors.name: AlreadyJoined
errors.msg: You have already joined this challenge

errors.name: WrongChallengeType
errors.msg: This operation is not valid for the challenge type (PVP vs TEAM mismatch)

errors.name: AlreadyClaimed
errors.msg: Winnings have already been claimed for this challenge

errors.name: NotAWinner
errors.msg: You are not on the winning team

errors.name: NotSettled
errors.msg: Challenge has not been settled yet

errors.name: NoWinningSide
errors.msg: The winning side has not been determined (no participants on one side)

errors.name: OpponentsJoined
errors.msg: Cannot cancel: one or more opponents have already joined this challenge

errors.name: NotCancelled
errors.msg: Challenge has not been cancelled

errors.name: NotEligibleForRefund
errors.msg: You are not eligible for a refund (you are not on the creator's team)

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Only the platform admin can perform this action

errors.name: InvalidParam
errors.msg: Invalid parameter value

errors.name: NotOpenOrActive
errors.msg: Challenge is already settled or cancelled; nothing to cancel

errors.name: InvalidWithdrawAmount
errors.msg: Withdrawal amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: InsufficientVaultBalance
errors.msg: Withdrawal amount exceeds the vault's balance

errors.name: UnauthorizedWithdrawAuthority
errors.msg: Only the designated withdraw authority may perform this action

types.name: AcceptChallengeParams
docs: `side` is only meaningful for TEAM challenges:
- `true`  → join the **creator's** side
- `false` → join the **opponent's** side
For PVP challenges `side` is ignored; the caller becomes the single challenger.

fields.name: join_creator_side
docs: TEAM only: which side to join (true = creator's side, false = opponent's side).
Ignored for PVP.

fields.name: amount
docs: Amount this participant deposits, in USDC micro-units. PVP participants
must match the creator's stake. TEAM participants must match it until the
opponent side has collectively covered it, after which the platform minimum applies.

types.name: ChallengeAccount
docs: The main challenge account stored on-chain.
PVP seeds:  [b"challenge", creator.key(), challenge_id.to_le_bytes()]
TEAM seeds: same — challenge_type differentiates behaviour at runtime.
Sizing note: this struct does NOT use `#[derive(InitSpace)]` /
`#[max_len(..)]`. The four TEAM-roster vecs below are the overwhelming
majority of this account's bytes (up to ~4KB at the platform-wide
`MAX_TEAM_SIZE` ceiling), and a PVP challenge — or a small TEAM one —
never needs that much room. Reserving the worst case on every challenge
means admin (the `fee_payer` in `create_challenge`) pays multiple times
more rent than necessary for the common case. Instead, `space_for` below
computes exactly the bytes needed for the roster capacity a given
challenge was actually created with — see
`instructions::create_challenge::effective_team_size`.

fields.name: creator
docs: The wallet that created the challenge

fields.name: rent_payer
docs: The wallet that paid the SOL rent for this challenge account + vault at
creation — either `creator` self-paying (if they had enough SOL) or
`config.admin` sponsoring. Every later close instruction
(`settle_challenge`, `cancel_challenge`, `admin_cancel_challenge`,
`claim_winnings`, `claim_refund`) refunds reclaimed rent to this same
wallet, not unconditionally to admin.

fields.name: challenger
docs: PVP only: the single wallet that accepted the challenge (zero if not yet accepted)

fields.name: challenger_bet_amount
docs: PVP only: the amount the challenger actually deposited (may differ from
the creator's `bet_amount`; zero until accepted).

fields.name: creator_team
docs: TEAM only: participants who joined the creator's side (creator is implicitly included).
Capacity is fixed at `init` time to this challenge's own `max_team_size`
(see `ChallengeAccount::space_for`), not the platform-wide `MAX_TEAM_SIZE`.

fields.name: creator_team_amounts
docs: TEAM only: amount each `creator_team` participant deposited, parallel to `creator_team`

fields.name: opponent_team
docs: TEAM only: participants who joined the opponent's side. Same per-challenge
capacity note as `creator_team`.

fields.name: opponent_team_amounts
docs: TEAM only: amount each `opponent_team` participant deposited, parallel to `opponent_team`

fields.name: max_team_size
docs: TEAM only: maximum participants per side (0 = no limit up to 50)

fields.name: winning_side
docs: TEAM only: which side won (set during settle_challenge)

fields.name: winning_side_total_amount
docs: TEAM only: combined stake of the winning side, snapshotted at settle time
(creator's bet_amount + creator_team_amounts if creator won, else opponent_team_amounts).
Used by claim_winnings to compute each winner's proportional share.

fields.name: settled_net_pot
docs: TEAM only: net pot (total stake across both sides, minus platform fee),
snapshotted at settle time and split proportionally in claim_winnings.

fields.name: winners_remaining
docs: TEAM only: number of winning-side participants who still need to call
claim_winnings, snapshotted at settle time and decremented on each claim.
When it hits zero the vault and this account are closed, returning their
rent to the admin wallet that originally paid it.

fields.name: challenge_type
docs: Whether this is a PVP or TEAM challenge

fields.name: challenge_id
docs: Unique numeric ID for this challenge (per creator)

fields.name: asset
docs: Asset symbol, e.g. "BTC", "SOL", "ETH". Capped at 10 bytes — see
`ChallengeAccount::FIXED_SPACE`, which reserves exactly 4 + 10 for it.

fields.name: bet_amount
docs: The creator's own stake, in USDC micro-units. Other participants may deposit a
different amount when they accept/join (see `challenger_bet_amount`,
`creator_team_amounts`, `opponent_team_amounts`); MIN_BET_AMOUNT still applies to all.

fields.name: target_price_usd_cents
docs: Target price in USD cents (e.g. $66,500 → 6_650_000)

fields.name: direction
docs: Direction of the creator's prediction

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Unix timestamp when the challenge expires (no more accepts/joins after this)

fields.name: resolves_at
docs: Unix timestamp when the price is evaluated (end of prediction window)

fields.name: status
docs: Current status

fields.name: vault_bump
docs: PDA bump for the vault

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump for this account

types.name: ChallengeStatus
docs: On-chain status of a challenge

types.name: ChallengeType
docs: Whether the challenge is 1-vs-1 or team-based

types.name: ClaimRecord
docs: Tracks whether a TEAM participant has already claimed their winnings.
Seeds: [b"claim", challenge.key(), participant.key()]
Created (init) when the participant calls claim_winnings for the first time.
Its existence is the double-claim guard.

types.name: Config
docs: Global platform configuration — a single singleton PDA.
Seeds: [b"config"]
`admin` is the live source of truth for who sponsors SOL rent/fees and is
authorised to settle challenges and update these parameters — set once at
`initialize_config`, changeable afterwards via `update_admin`. The other
fields mirror the compile-time defaults in `constants.rs` but can be
tuned at runtime via `update_platform_params` without a program redeploy.
`platform_fee_bps` and `creator_fee_bps` are both deducted from the pot
at settlement — the former to the platform treasury, the latter to
`challenge.creator` regardless of who wins.

fields.name: asset
docs: Asset symbol, e.g. "BTC"

fields.name: bet_amount
docs: Bet amount in USDC micro-units per participant (6 decimals, e.g. $5 = 5_000_000)

fields.name: target_price_usd_cents
docs: Target price in USD cents (e.g. $66,500 → 6_650_000)

fields.name: direction_above
docs: true = creator predicts price will be ABOVE target; false = BELOW

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Unix timestamp: last moment another user can accept / join this challenge

fields.name: resolves_at
docs: Unix timestamp: when the price outcome is evaluated

fields.name: challenge_type
docs: PVP = single opponent; Team = any number of participants per side

fields.name: max_team_size
docs: TEAM only: max participants per side (0 = platform default, capped at
the live `Config::max_team_size`, itself capped at `MAX_TEAM_SIZE` = 50).
Ignored for PVP challenges.
This directly determines how many bytes the `challenge` account is
allocated with (see `effective_team_size` / `ChallengeAccount::space_for`),
and therefore how much SOL rent `fee_payer` (admin) pays to create it —
a small `max_team_size` costs proportionally less than a large one.

types.name: CreatorCounter
docs: A per-creator counter so each creator can have multiple challenges.
Seeds: [b"creator_counter", creator.key()]

types.name: PredictionDirection
docs: Direction of the price prediction

types.name: UpdatePlatformParamsArgs
docs: All fields optional — only the ones provided are updated.

types.name: WinningSide
docs: Which side won a TEAM challenge (stored after settlement so claim_winnings can verify)

types.name: WithdrawAuthority
docs: Dedicated authority allowed to call `admin_withdraw`, kept separate from
`Config::admin` so a leaked/compromised admin hot wallet (the one loaded
into the settlement service) cannot on its own drain escrowed vaults.
Seeds: [b"withdraw_authority"]
Bootstrapped once via `initialize_withdraw_authority` (admin-gated);
after that only this authority can rotate itself via
`update_withdraw_authority` — `Config::admin` has no path to reassign it.
